techniques.name: 5-4-3-2-1 Technique
techniques.description: Use your five senses to ground yourself in the present moment
steps: Name 5 things you can SEE around you
Name 4 things you can TOUCH
Name 3 things you can HEAR
Name 2 things you can SMELL
Name 1 thing you can TASTE
techniques.benefits: Helps reduce anxiety, panic attacks, and overwhelming emotions

techniques.name: Box Breathing (4-4-4-4)
techniques.description: A simple breathing technique to calm your nervous system
steps: Breathe in slowly for 4 counts
Hold your breath for 4 counts
Breathe out slowly for 4 counts
Hold your breath out for 4 counts
Repeat this cycle 4-8 times
techniques.benefits: Reduces stress, improves focus, and activates relaxation response

techniques.name: Mindful Breathing
techniques.description: Focus your attention on your breath to center yourself
steps: Find a comfortable position and close your eyes
Focus on the sensation of breathing
Notice the rise and fall of your chest or abdomen
If your mind wanders, gently return focus to your breath
Continue for 5-10 minutes
techniques.benefits: Improves emotional regulation, reduces stress, and increases mindfulness

techniques.name: Safe Place Visualization
techniques.description: Create a mental sanctuary where you feel calm and secure
steps: Close your eyes and imagine a place where you feel completely safe
Use all your senses - what do you see, hear, smell, feel?
Add details that make this place more real and comforting
Imagine yourself there, feeling peaceful and protected
Stay in this place for a few minutes, breathing deeply
techniques.benefits: Provides emotional refuge, reduces anxiety, and promotes inner peace

techniques.name: Body Scan Meditation
techniques.description: Progressive relaxation technique to release tension and increase body awareness
steps: Lie down or sit comfortably in a quiet space
Start at your toes and slowly move attention up through your body
Notice any tension, warmth, or sensations without judgment
Breathe into areas of tension and imagine them releasing
Continue until you've scanned your entire body to the top of your head
Rest for a few moments before slowly returning to the present
techniques.benefits: Releases physical tension, improves body awareness, and promotes deep relaxation

techniques.name: Progressive Muscle Relaxation
techniques.description: Systematically tense and relax muscle groups to reduce physical stress
steps: Find a comfortable seated or lying position
Start with your feet - tense the muscles for 5 seconds
Release the tension and notice the feeling of relaxation
Move up to your calves, thighs, abdomen, hands, arms, shoulders, and face
For each muscle group, tense for 5 seconds then release for 10 seconds
Finish by taking three deep breaths and stretching gently
techniques.benefits: Reduces muscle tension, lowers stress hormones, and improves sleep quality

techniques.name: Physical Grounding with Objects
techniques.description: Use physical objects to anchor yourself in the present moment
steps: Choose a small object you can hold (stone, stress ball, textured fabric)
Focus all your attention on the object's physical properties
Notice its temperature, weight, texture, and shape
Describe the object in detail to yourself or out loud
Move it between your hands and explore different sensations
Continue for 2-3 minutes or until you feel more grounded
techniques.benefits: Provides tactile distraction from anxiety and anchors awareness to the present

techniques.name: 4-7-8 Breathing Technique
techniques.description: A powerful breathing pattern to activate the relaxation response quickly
steps: Sit comfortably with your back straight
Place the tip of your tongue against the ridge behind your upper teeth
Exhale completely through your mouth making a whoosh sound
Close your mouth and inhale quietly through your nose for 4 counts
Hold your breath for 7 counts
Exhale completely through your mouth for 8 counts making a whoosh sound
Repeat the cycle 3-4 times
techniques.benefits: Rapidly calms the nervous system, reduces anxiety, and helps with sleep

tips.title: Practice Regularly
tips.description: Try to practice grounding techniques daily, even when you're feeling good.

tips.title: Find What Works
tips.description: Different techniques work for different people and situations.

tips.title: Be Patient
tips.description: Grounding techniques are skills that improve with practice.

tips.title: Be Kind to Yourself
tips.description: If your mind wanders during practice, gently guide it back without judgment.
